entry.title: 2.11. Supplement
entry.content:
Non-restrictive ### The Strange Situation Experiment, conducted by psychologist Mary Ainsworth in 1970s, determined the attachment behavior of infants toward their caregivers. * Subject * Head: The Strange Situation Experiment * Supplement(Past-Participial Clause): conducted by psychologist Mary Ainsworth in 1970s * Head: conducted * Complement(PP): by psychologist Mary Ainsworth * Modifier(PP): in 1970s * Predicate * Head: determined * Complement: the attachment behavior of infants toward their caregivers * Determiner: the * Modifier(NP): attachment * Head: behavior * Subject Corresponding Complement(PP): of infants * Modifier(PP): toward their caregivers
> Simplification: The Strange Situation Experiment, conducted / by psychologist Mary Ainsworth / in 1970s, determined / the attachment behavior / of infants / toward their caregivers.
entry.summary: Non-restrictive The Strange Situation Experiment, conducted by psychologist Mary Ainsworth in 1970s, determined the attachment behavior of infants toward their caregivers. Subject Head: The Strange Situation Experiment Supplement(Past-Participial Clause): conducted by psychologist Mary Ainsworth in 1970s Head: conducted Complement(PP): by psychologist Mary Ainsworth Modifier(PP): in 1970s Predicate Head: determined Complement: the attachment behavior of infants toward their caregivers Determiner: the Modifier(NP): attachment Head: behavior Subject Corresponding Complement(PP): of infants Modifier(PP): toward their caregivers

entry.title: 2.9. Dependent in NP
entry.content:
기본적으로 modifier이고, head noun이 특별히 license하는 경우 complement일 수도 있다.
## 1. Modifier
## 2. Complement 대체로 verb와의 파생 관계(derivational relation)를 반영한다. 따라서 대부분의 경우 object corresponding이다. ### 2.1. Object Corresponding Complement #### the construction of the dam #### abandonment of his ship #### criticism of her decision #### abandonment of his ship
### 2.2. Subject Corresponding Complement #### the rise of the sun * Determiner(D): the * Head(N): rise * Subject Corresponding Complement(PP): of the sun * Head: of * Complement: the sun
entry.summary: 기본적으로 modifier이고, head noun이 특별히 license하는 경우 complement일 수도 있다.

entry.title: 2.5. To-Infinitival Clause
entry.content:
## 1. Complement로 기능하는 To-Infinitival Clause ### I want to drink cold water. * Subject(NP): I * Head(N): I * Predicate(VP): want to drink cold water * Head(Lexical Verb): want * Complement(to-infinitival Clause): to drink cold water * Predicand: I * Predicate(VP): drink cold water * Head(Lexical Verb): drink * Direct Object(NP): cold water * Modifier(AdjP): cold * Head(Attributive Adj): cold * Head(N): water
> Simplification: I / want / to drink cold water.
### He seems to be incorrect. * Raised Subject(NP): He * Head(N): He * Predicate(VP): seems to be incorrect * Head(Lexical Verb): seems * Complement(to-infinitival Clause): to be incorrect * Predicand: he * Predicate(VP): be incorrect * Head(Auxiliary Verb): be * Complement(AdjP): incorrect * Head(Predicative Adj): incorrect
> Simplification: seems / he to be incorrect.
### I expect him to pass the exam. * Subject(NP): I * Head(N): I * Predicate: expect him to pass the exam * Head(Auxiliary Verb): expect * Complement(to-infinitival Clause): him to pass the exam * Subject(NP): him * Head(N): him * Predicate(VP): pass the exam * Head(Lexical Verb): pass * Direct Object(NP): the exam * Determiner(DP): the * Head(D): the * Head(N): exam
### I was glad to be invited. * Subject(NP): I * Head(N): I * Predicate(VP): was glad to be invited * Head(Auxiliary Verb): was * Predicative Complement(AdjP): glad to be invited * Head(Predicative Adj): glad * Complement(to-infinitival clause): to be invited * Predicand: I * Head(Auxiliary Verb): be * Complement(subjectless non-finite clause): invited * Predicand: I * Predicate(VP): invited * Head(Lexical Verb): invited
> I / was / glad / to be invited.
### I want you to stay. * Subject: I * Predicate: want you to stay * Head: want * Raised Object: you * Complement(To-Infinitival Clause): to stay * Subject: you * Predicate: to stay
> I / want / you to stay.
---
## 2. Modifier로 기능하는 To-Infinitival Clause ### We have little time to waste. * Subject: We * Head: We * Predicate: have little time to waste. * Head: have * Direct Object(NP): little time to waste * Determiner(DP): little * Head(NP): time to waste * Head: time * Modifier(To-Infinitival Clause): to waste * Predicand: we * Head: waste
> Simplification: We / have / little time to waste.
---
## 3. Adjunct로 기능하는 To-Infinitival Clause ### For Mike to succeed, he must improve his study habit. * Adjunct(To-Infinitival Clause): For mike to succeed * Subject: Mike * Predicate: to succeed * Subject: he * Predicate(VP): must improve his study habit * Head(VP): must * Head(Auxiliary Verb): must * Complement(Bare Infinitival Clause): improve his study habit * Predicand: he * Predicate(VP): improve his study habit * Head(V): improve * Direct Object(NP): his study habit * Determiner(Genitive NP): his * Head(Nominal): study habit * Modifier(NP): study * Head(N): habit
> Simplification: For Mike / to succeed, he / must / improve his study habit.
entry.summary: 1. Complement로 기능하는 To-Infinitival Clause I want to drink cold water. Subject(NP): I Head(N): I Predicate(VP): want to drink cold water Head(Lexical Verb): want Complement(to-infinitival Clause): to drink cold water Predicand: I Predicate(VP): drink cold water Head(Lexical Verb): drink Direct Object(NP): cold water Modifier(AdjP): cold Head(Attributive Adj): cold Head(N): water

entry.title: 2.6. Bare Infinitival Clause
entry.content:
## 1. Lexical Verb의 Complement로 기능하는 Bare Infinitival Clause ### She made the boys stay after school. * Subject: She * Predicate: * Head: made * Raised Object: the boys * Complement(Bare Infinitival Clause): stay after school * Predicand: the boys * Head: stay * Adjunct(PP): after school
> Simplification: She / made / the boys stay after school.
### I didn't see you come in. * Predicate: * Head: didn't * Complement(VP): see you come in * Head: see * Raised Object: * Complement(Bare Infinitival Clause): (you) come in * Predicand: you * Predicate: come in
> Simplification: I / didn't / see / you come in.
--- ## 2. Auxiliary Verb의 Complement로 기능하는 Bare Infinitival Clause ### For Mike to succeed, he must improve his study habit. * Adjunct(To-Infinitival Clause): For mike to succeed * Subject: Mike * Predicate(VP): succeed * Subject: he * Predicate(VP): must improve his study habit * Head(VP): must * Head(Auxiliary Verb): must * Complement(Bare Infinitival Clause): improve his study habit * Predicand: he * Predicate(VP): improve his study habit * Head(V): improve * Direct Object(NP): his study habit * Determiner(Genitive NP): his * Head(Nominal): study habit * Modifier(NP): study * Head(N): habit
entry.summary: 1. Lexical Verb의 Complement로 기능하는 Bare Infinitival Clause She made the boys stay after school. Subject: She Predicate: Head: made Raised Object: the boys Complement(Bare Infinitival Clause): stay after school Predicand: the boys Head: stay Adjunct(PP): after school

entry.title: 2.7. Gerund-Participial Clause
entry.content:
## 1. Subject로 기능하는 Gerund-Participial Clause ### Learning Chinese is easy. * Subject(Gerund-Participial Clause): learning Chinese * Predicand: we * Predicate: learning Chinese * Head: learning * Direct Object: Chinese * Predicate: is easy * Head: is * Complement: easy
> Simplification: Learning Chinese / is / easy
---
## 2. Complement로 기능하는 Gerund-Participial Clause ### She likes dancing. * Subject: she * Predicate * Head: likes * Complement(Gerund-Participial Clause): dancing * Predicand: she * Predicate: dancing
> Simplification: She / likes / dancing.
### I'm sorry for giving you trouble. * Subject: I * Predicate * Head(Auxiliary Verb): am * Complement(AdjP): sorry for giving you trouble * Head(Adj): sorry * Complement(PP): for giving you trouble * Head(P): for * Complement(Gerund-Participial Clause): giving you trouble * Predicand: I * Predicae: giving you trouble * Head: giving * Indirect Object: you * Direct Object: trouble
> Simplification: I / am / sorry / for giving you trouble.
### She is tired of Lucy's being late. * Subject: she * Predicate * Head(Auxiliary Verb): is * Complement(AdjP): tired of Lucy's being late * Head: tired * Complement(PP): of Lucy's being late * Head(P): of * Complement(Gerund-Participial Clause): Lucy's being late * Subject(NP) * Head(Genitive N): Lucy's * Predicate: being late * Head(Auxiliary Verb): being * Complement(AdjP): late
> Simplification: She / is / tired / of Lucy's being late.
### It's nice seeing him again. * Predicate * Head(Auxiliary Verb): is * Complement(AdjP): nice seeing him again * Head(Adj): nice * Predicand(Gerund-Participial Clause): seeing him again * Predicate: seeing him again * Head(V): seeing * Direct Object: him * Modifier(AdjP): again * Predicand: I
> Simplification: It is / nice / seeing him again. (의미: situation 평가)
## 3. Supplement로 기능하는 Gerund-Participial Clause ### Smiling brightly, the girl turned toward him. * Supplement(Gerund-Participial Clause): smiling brightly * Predicand: the girl * Predicate: smiling brightly * Head: smiling * Modifier(AdvP): brightly * Matrix Clause: the girl turned toward him * Subject: the girl * Predicate: turned toward him * Head: turned * Complement(PP): toward him * Head(P): toward * Object(NP): him
> Simplification: Smiling brightly, the girl / turned toward him.
entry.summary: 1. Subject로 기능하는 Gerund-Participial Clause Learning Chinese is easy. Subject(Gerund-Participial Clause): learning Chinese Predicand: we Predicate: learning Chinese Head: learning Direct Object: Chinese Predicate: is easy Head: is Complement: easy

entry.title: 2.3. Clauses
entry.content:
# 1. Clause ## 1. Finite Clause * Property * 대체로 subject, tense, agreement를 갖는다. * 특정 시공간의 상황을 가리킬 수 있다.
## 2. Non-Finite Clause * Property * non-finite clause는 항상 subordinate이다. * tense나 agreement 정보를 보이지 않는다. * subjectless인 경우가 많다. (hollow인 경우도 있다.)
---
# 2. Non-Finite Clauses ## 특징적 구조 1. Subjectless predicate뿐 아니라 subjectless non-finite clauses도 predicand를 가져야 한다. 2. Hollow
## Categories 1. To-Infinitival 2. Bare Infinitival 3. Gerund-Participial 4. Past-Participial 5. Verbless
entry.summary: 1. Clause 1. Finite Clause Property 대체로 subject, tense, agreement를 갖는다. 특정 시공간의 상황을 가리킬 수 있다.

entry.title: 2.13. Extraposition, Hollow Clause
entry.content:
## 1. Extraposition ### It's good to get up early. * Predicate(VP): is good * Head(Auxiliary Verb): is * Predicative Complement(AdjP): good * Predicand(To-Infinitival Clause): to get up early * Predicate: get up early * Head(VP): get up * Modifier(AdvP): early * Predicand: we
--- ## 2. Hollow Non-Finite Clause *Gap의 해석이 matrix clause 전체와 관련이 있기 때문에 hollow는 predicate의 성질보다는 matrix clause의 성질로 본다고 한다. ### Here is a new book for you to read. * Predicate: Here is * Head: is * Locative Complement(AdvP): Here * Subject(NP): a new book for you to read. * Determiner(DP): a * Head(NP): new book for you to read * Head(N): book * Modifier(AdjP): new * Modifier(Hollow To-Infinitival Clause): for you to read * Subject: you * Predicate(VP): read * Head: read * Direct Object Gap: book
> Simplification: Here / is / a new book / for you to read. ### These students are very difficult for us to teach. * Subject(NP): These students * Determiner(DP): These * Head(N): students * Predicate(VP): are very difficult for us to teach * Head(Auxiliary Verb): are * Predicative Complement(AdjP): very difficult for us to teach * Modifier(AdvP): very * Head(Adj): difficult * Complement(Hollow To-Infinitival Clause): for us to teach * Subject: us * Predicate(VP): teach * Head(V): teach * Direct Object Gap(NP): these students
> Simplification: These students / are / very difficult [for us to teach].
entry.summary: 1. Extraposition It’s good to get up early. Predicate(VP): is good Head(Auxiliary Verb): is Predicative Complement(AdjP): good Predicand(To-Infinitival Clause): to get up early Predicate: get up early Head(VP): get up Modifier(AdvP): early Predicand: we

entry.title: Sentences
entry.content:
## 1. Action 1. $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act}, \text{Theme}\rangle$ *$\text{Agent}$는 의지를 가지고 동작을 수행하는 주체 2. $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act},\text{Patient}\rangle$ *$\text{Patient}$는 단순 대상이 아니라 내부적으로 상태가 변화하는 대상 3. $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act},\text{Proposition}\rangle$
> Examples. > 1. The company exported rice to Japan. > 2. He ate the apple. She painted the wall. > 3. The teacher recommended that she be sent home.
## 2. Experience 1. $\langle\text{Experiencer},\text{Experience},\text{Stimulus}\rangle$ *의지적 작용이 아니라 심리적, 감각적 인지를 경험하는 주체 2. $\langle\text{Experiencer},\text{Experience},\text{Proposition}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. I feel cold. She loves music. >2. I believe she will come home.
## 3. Attribution 1. $\langle\text{Object}, \text{Function}\rangle$ 2. $\langle\text{Object}, \text{State}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. This key opens the back door. >2. I was hospitalized. English is spoken in many countries. The data is being processed. The road leads to the city center.
## 4. Deontic 1. $\langle\text{Rule}, \text{Constraint},\text{Situation}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. The law forbids smoking in this area.
## 5. Causation 1. $\langle\text{Cause}, \text{Effect}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. The cold froze the river. The music makes me happy.
## 6. Indication 1. $\langle\text{Evidence}, \text{Indication}, \text{Proposition}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. The study suggests that caffeine improves memory.
## 7. Conceptual 1. $\langle\text{Term}, \text{Definition}\rangle$ 2. $\langle\text{Term}, \text{Classification}\rangle$ 3. $\langle\text{Term}, \text{Property}\rangle$
>Examples. >1. A square is a rectangle with four sides of equal length. >2. Mammalia belongs to the phylum Chordata. >3. Mercury has a very high surface tension.
entry.summary: 1. Action $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act}, \text{Theme}\rangle$ *$\text{Agent}$는 의지를 가지고 동작을 수행하는 주체 $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act},\text{Patient}\rangle$ *$\text{Patient}$는 단순 대상이 아니라 내부적으로 상태가 변화하는 대상 $\langle\text{Agent},\text{Act},\text{Proposition}\rangle$

entry.title: Auxiliary Verb
entry.content:
### 1. can 1. 능력 2. 허락 3. **가능성** 4. **추측**
>Examples. >1. He can lift 500N. >2. You can go home now. >3. Even expert drivers can make mistakes. >4. She can't be tired now.
### 2. may 1. 허락 2. 추측
### 3. must, have to 1. 의무 2. **필요** 3. 강한 추측
>Examples. >1. You must do military service. >2. I have to call my mom. >3. He must be hungry.
### 4. will 1. **의지** 2. **cooperativeness** 3. **disposition(경향)** 4. **습성(습관적)** 5. **확실한 추측**
>Examples. >1. I will lose weight. >2. The door won't open. >3. After sad things, happy things will happen. (슬픈 일 뒤에는 행복한일이 생기기 마련이다.) >4. I would sometimes travel alone. (나는 때로 혼자 여행을 하곤 했다.) >5. He will be a doctor.
### 5. should, ought to 1. 의무 2. **합리적 추측**
